            "text": "async (req, res) =&gt; {",

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 2068
          },
          {

          },
          {

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "  // Parent selection state",

          },
          {
            "text": "  const [parentSearch, setParentSearch] = useState('');",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [parentCompanies, setParentCompanies] = useState([]);",

          },
          {
            "text": "  const [parentPagination, setParentPagination] = useState({",

          },
          {

          },

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "  const debouncedSearch = useDebounce(parentSearch, 500);",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "  const fetchParentCompanies = useCallback(async (page = 1, search = '') =&gt; {",

          },
          {

          },
          {

          },

          },

            "text": "const Branches = ",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "  // Filter branches based on search and status",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "  useEffect(() =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "    let filtered = [...branches];",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "    // Search filter - only filter if 3+ characters",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (searchQuery.trim().length &gt;= 3) {",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "      const query = searchQuery.toLowerCase();",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "      filtered = filtered.filter(",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "        (branch) =&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "          branch.name.toLowerCase().includes(query) ||",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "          (branch.code &amp;&amp; branch.code.toLowerCase().includes(query)) ||",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "          (branch.email &amp;&amp; branch.email.toLowerCase().includes(query)) ||",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "          (branch.referenceNo &amp;&amp; branch.referenceNo.toString().includes(query))",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "      );",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "    // Status filter",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (statusFilter !== 'all') {",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "      filtered = filtered.filter(",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "        (branch) =&gt; (statusFilter === 'active' &amp;&amp; branch.isActive) || (statusFilter === 'inactive' &amp;&amp; !branch.isActive)",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "      );",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "text": "    setFilteredBranches(filtered);",
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "  }, [branches, searchQuery, statusFilter]);",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "  const fetchBranches = async () =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "    try {",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "      setLoading(true);",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "      const response = await api.get('/api/companies', {",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "        params: {",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "text": "          type: 'BRANCH'",
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "      });",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "      setBranches(response.data);",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "text": "    } catch (error) {",
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "text": "      console.error('Error fetching branches:', error);",
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "text": "      toast.error('Failed to fetch branches: ' + (error.response?.data?.message || error.message));",
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "text": "    } finally {",
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "text": "      setLoading(false);",
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "text": "  };",
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "text": "  const fetchParentCompanies = async () =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "    try {",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "      // Fetch all companies that can be parents (CORP and SUB)",
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "      const response = await api.get('/api/companies');",
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "text": "      const parents = response.data.filter(c =&gt; c.type === 'CORP' || c.type === 'SUB');",
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "text": "      setParentCompanies(parents);",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "    } catch (error) {",

          },
          {

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {

        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "const AddBranch = ",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "  const handleInputChange = (e) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "    const { name, value, type, checked } = e.target;",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (name.startsWith('address.')) {",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "      const addressField = name.split('.')[1];",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "      setFormData(prev =&gt; ({",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "        ...prev,",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "        address: {",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "          ...prev.address,",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "          [addressField]: value",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "      }));",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "    } else {",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "text": "      setFormData(prev =&gt; ({",
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "        ...prev,",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "text": "        [name]: type === 'checkbox' ? checked : value",
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "      }));",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "  };",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "  const handleParentSelect = (parent) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "    setSelectedParent(parent);",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "    setFormData(prev =&gt; ({",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "text": "      ...prev,",
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "      parentId: parent.id",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "    }));",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "  };",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "text": "  const handleParentPageChange = (newPage) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "text": "    fetchParentCompanies(newPage, debouncedSearch);",
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "text": "  };",
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "text": "  const handleSubmit = async (e) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "text": "    e.preventDefault();",
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (!formData.name.trim()) {",
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "      toast.error('Company name is required');",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "      return;",
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (!formData.parentId) {",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "      toast.error('Please select a parent company');",
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "text": "      return;",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "    try {",
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "text": "      setLoading(true);",
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "text": "      const payload = {",
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "text": "        name: formData.name.trim(),",
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "text": "        type: 'BRANCH',",
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "text": "        referenceNo: formData.referenceNo ? parseInt(formData.referenceNo) : null,",
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "text": "        edi: formData.edi,",
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "text": "        parentId: formData.parentId,",
            "lineNumber": 115
          },
          {
            "text": "        address: formData.address",
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "text": "      };",
            "lineNumber": 117
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "text": "      await api.post('/api/companies', payload);",
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "text": "      toast.success('Branch created successfully!');",
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "text": "      navigate('/companies');",
            "lineNumber": 121
          },
          {
            "text": "    } catch (error) {",
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "text": "      toast.error('Error creating branch: ' + (error.response?.data?.message || error.message));",
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "text": "    } finally {",
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "text": "      setLoading(false);",
            "lineNumber": 125
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {

          },

            "lineNumber": 128
          },

            "lineNumber": 130
          },

        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "const Companies = ",

            "text": "const handleCreateCompany = async ",

          },
          {
            "text": " catch (error) {",

          {

            "lineNumber": 771

            "text": "    } finally {",

          },

            "lineNumber": 774
          },
          {
            "text": "  };",
            "lineNumber": 775
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 776
          },
          {
            "text": "  const getTypeLabel = (type) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 777
          },
          {
            "text": "    const labels = {",
            "lineNumber": 778
          },
          {
            "text": "      'CORP': 'Corporate',",
            "lineNumber": 779
          },
          {
            "text": "      'SUB': 'Subsidiary',",
            "lineNumber": 780
          },
          {
            "text": "      'BRANCH': 'Branch'",
            "lineNumber": 781
          },
          {
            "text": "    };",
            "lineNumber": 782
          },
          {
            "text": "    return labels[type] || type || 'N/A';",

          },
          {
            "text": "  };",
            "lineNumber": 784
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 785
          },
          {
            "text": "  const getTypeBadgeClass = (type) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 786
          },
          {
            "text": "    const classes = {",

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "text": "      'SUB': 'bg-info-lt',",
            "lineNumber": 789
          },
          {
            "text": "      'BRANCH': 'bg-success-lt'",
            "lineNumber": 790
          },
          {
            "text": "    };",
            "lineNumber": 791
          },
          {
            "text": "    return classes[type] || 'bg-secondary-lt';",

          },
          {
            "text": "  };",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 794
          },
          {
            "text": "  const getParentCompany = (company) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 795
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (company.parent) {",
            "lineNumber": 796
          },
          {
            "text": "      return company.parent;",
            "lineNumber": 797
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 798
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (company.parentId) {",
            "lineNumber": 799
          },
          {

          },

          },
          {

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 804
          },
          {
            "text": "  // Check if company has children",

          },
          {
            "text": "  const hasChildren = (company) =&gt; {",

          },
          {
            "text": "    return companies.some(c =&gt; c.parentId === company.id);",
            "lineNumber": 807
          },
          {
            "text": "  };",
            "lineNumber": 808
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 809
          },
          {
            "text": "  // Load relationships for modal (lazy load)",

          },
          {
            "text": "  const loadModalRelationships = async (companyId) =&gt; {",

          },
          {

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "text": "      return;",

          },
          {
            "text": "    }",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 816
          },
          {
            "text": "    setLoadingModalRelationships(prev =&gt; ({ ...prev, [companyId]: true }));",

          },
          {
            "text": "    try {",
            "lineNumber": 818
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 819
          },
          {
            "text": "      setModalRelationships(prev =&gt; ({ ...prev, [companyId]: response.data }));",

          },
          {
            "text": "    } catch (error) {",

          },
          {

          },

          },

          },

        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "module.exports = (sequelize) =&gt; {",

          },

          },
          {

        },
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "const Companies = ",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "const renderModal = ",
            "lineNumber": 856
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 909
          },
          {
            "text": "                      &lt;div className=\"tab-content\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 910
          },
          {
            "text": "                        &lt;div className={&#96;tab-pane ${activeTab === 'basic' ? 'active show' : ''}&#96;} id=\"basic-info\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 911
          },
          {
            "text": "                          &lt;div className=\"row\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 912
          },
          {
            "text": "                            {/* Left Column - Company Details */}",
            "lineNumber": 913
          },
          {
            "text": "                            &lt;div className=\"col-md-6\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 914
          },
          {
            "text": "                              &lt;div className=\"mb-3\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 915
          },
          {
            "text": "                                &lt;label className=\"form-label required\"&gt;Company Type&lt;/label&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 916
          },
          {
            "text": "                                &lt;select",
            "lineNumber": 917
          },
          {
            "text": "                                  className=\"form-select\"",
            "lineNumber": 918
          },
          {
            "text": "                                  value={formData.type || 'CORP'}",
            "lineNumber": 919
          },
          {
            "text": "                                  onChange={(e) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 920
          },
          {
            "text": "                                    const newType = e.target.value;",
            "lineNumber": 921
          },
          {
            "text": "                                    setFormData(prev =&gt; ({",
            "lineNumber": 922
          },
          {
            "text": "                                      ...prev,",
            "lineNumber": 923
          },
          {
            "text": "                                      type: newType,",
            "lineNumber": 924
          },
          {
            "text": "                                      parentId: newType === 'CORP' ? null : prev.parentId",
            "lineNumber": 925
          },
          {
            "text": "                                    }));",
            "lineNumber": 926
          },
          {
            "text": "                                    if (newType !== 'CORP') fetchParentCompanies(1);",
            "lineNumber": 927
          },
          {
            "text": "                                  }}",
            "lineNumber": 928
          },
          {
            "text": "                                &gt;",
            "lineNumber": 929
          },
          {
            "text": "                                  &lt;option value=\"CORP\"&gt;Corporate&lt;/option&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 930
          },
          {
            "text": "                                  &lt;option value=\"SUB\"&gt;Subsidiary&lt;/option&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 931
          },
          {
            "text": "                                  &lt;option value=\"BRANCH\"&gt;Branch&lt;/option&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 932
          },
          {
            "text": "                                &lt;/select&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 933
          },
          {
            "text": "                                &lt;small className=\"form-hint\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 934
          },
          {
            "text": "                                  {currentType === 'CORP' &amp;&amp; 'Top-level company with no parent'}",
            "lineNumber": 935
          },
          {
            "text": "                                  {currentType === 'SUB' &amp;&amp; 'Subsidiary of a corporate company'}",
            "lineNumber": 936
          },
          {
            "text": "                                  {currentType === 'BRANCH' &amp;&amp; 'Branch of a corporate or subsidiary company'}",
            "lineNumber": 937
          },
          {
            "text": "                                &lt;/small&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 938
          },
          {
            "text": "                              &lt;/div&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 939
          },
          {
            "text": "                              &lt;div className=\"mb-3\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 940
          },
          {
            "text": "                                &lt;label className=\"form-label required\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 941
          },
          {
            "text": ";",
            "lineNumber": 1178

            "text": ";",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\frontend\\src\\pages\\Companies.js",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 10
          },
          "endPosition": {

        },
        "signatures": {
          "ranges": [
            {
              "startPosition": {
                "line": 11,
                "column": 1
              },
              "endPosition": {

            },
            {
              "startPosition": {
                "line": 11,
                "column": 7
              },
              "endPosition": {
                "line": 11,
                "column": 19
              }
            }
          ]
        },
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "const Companies = ",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 721
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 722
          },
          {
            "text": "  const handleCreateCompany = async (e) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 723
          },
          {
            "text": "    e.preventDefault();",
            "lineNumber": 724
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (!companyFormData.name.trim()) {",
            "lineNumber": 725
          },
          {
            "text": "      toast.error('Company name is required');",
            "lineNumber": 726
          },
          {
            "text": "      return;",
            "lineNumber": 727
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 728
          },
          {
            "text": "    const currentType = companyFormData.type || 'CORP';",
            "lineNumber": 729
          },
          {
            "text": "    const needsParent = currentType === 'SUB' || currentType === 'BRANCH';",
            "lineNumber": 730
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (!editingCompany &amp;&amp; needsParent &amp;&amp; !companyFormData.parentId) {",
            "lineNumber": 731
          },
          {
            "text": "      toast.error('Please select a parent company');",
            "lineNumber": 732
          },
          {
            "text": "      return;",
            "lineNumber": 733
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 734
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 735
          },
          {
            "text": "    try {",
            "lineNumber": 736
          },
          {
            "text": "      setCreating(true);",
            "lineNumber": 737
          },
          {
            "text": "      const payload = {",
            "lineNumber": 738
          },
          {
            "text": "        name: companyFormData.name.trim(),",
            "lineNumber": 739
          },
          {
            "text": "        type: currentType,",
            "lineNumber": 740
          },
          {
            "text": "        referenceNo: companyFormData.referenceNo ? parseInt(companyFormData.referenceNo) : null,",
            "lineNumber": 741
          },
          {
            "text": "        edi: companyFormData.edi,",
            "lineNumber": 742
          },
          {
            "text": "        globalSystemEmail: companyFormData.globalSystemEmail || null,",
            "lineNumber": 743
          },
          {
            "text": "        primaryContactId: companyFormData.primaryContactId || null,",
            "lineNumber": 744
          },
          {
            "text": "        sendInvoiceEmail: companyFormData.sendInvoiceEmail || false,",
            "lineNumber": 745
          },
          {
            "text": "        sendInvoiceAttachment: companyFormData.sendInvoiceAttachment || false,",
            "lineNumber": 746
          },
          {
            "text": "        sendStatementEmail: companyFormData.sendStatementEmail || false,",
            "lineNumber": 747
          },
          {
            "text": "        sendStatementAttachment: companyFormData.sendStatementAttachment || false,",
            "lineNumber": 748
          },
          {
            "text": "        sendEmailAsSummary: companyFormData.sendEmailAsSummary || false,",
            "lineNumber": 749
          },
          {
            "text": "        isActive: editingCompany ? companyFormData.isActive : true,",
            "lineNumber": 750
          },
          {
            "text": "        address: companyFormData.address",
            "lineNumber": 751
          },
          {
            "text": "      };",
            "lineNumber": 752
          },
          {
            "text": "      if (needsParent) {",
            "lineNumber": 753
          },
          {
            "text": "        payload.parentId = companyFormData.parentId;",
            "lineNumber": 754
          },
          {
            "text": "      } else if (editingCompany) {",
            "lineNumber": 755
          },
          {
            "text": "        payload.parentId = null;",
            "lineNumber": 756
          },
          {
            "text": "      }",
            "lineNumber": 757
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 758
          },
          {
            "text": "      if (editingCompany) {",
            "lineNumber": 759
          },
          {
            "text": "        await api.put(&#96;/api/companies/${editingCompany.id}&#96;, payload);",
            "lineNumber": 760
          },
          {
            "text": "        toast.success('Company updated successfully!');",
            "lineNumber": 761
          },
          {
            "text": "      } else {",
            "lineNumber": 762
          },
          {
            "text": "        await api.post('/api/companies', payload);",
            "lineNumber": 763
          },
          {
            "text": "        toast.success('Company created successfully!');",
            "lineNumber": 764
          },
          {
            "text": "      }",
            "lineNumber": 765
          },
          {
            "text": "      setShowCompanyModal(false);",
            "lineNumber": 766
          },
          {
            "text": "      resetCompanyForm();",
            "lineNumber": 767
          },
          {
            "text": "      setEditingCompany(null);",
            "lineNumber": 768
          },
          {
            "text": "      fetchCompanies();",
            "lineNumber": 769
          },
          {
            "text": "    } catch (error) {",
            "lineNumber": 770
          },
          {
            "text": "      toast.error(&#96;Error ${editingCompany ? 'updating' : 'creating'} company: &#96; + (error.response?.data?.message || error.message));",
            "lineNumber": 771
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 772
          },
          {
            "text": ";",
            "lineNumber": 775
          },
          {
            "text": ";",
            "lineNumber": 2197
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.40539801120758057
    },

          },

          },

          },

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "text": "          }",

          },

          },
          {

          },
          {

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 169
          },
          {

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\frontend\\src\\pages\\Branches.js",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 59
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 618,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "import api from '../services/api';",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "import toast from '../utils/toast';",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "const Branches = () =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [branches, setBranches] = useState([]);",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [filteredBranches, setFilteredBranches] = useState([]);",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [showModal, setShowModal] = useState(false);",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [selectedBranch, setSelectedBranch] = useState(null);",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState('');",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [statusFilter, setStatusFilter] = useState('all');",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [parentCompanies, setParentCompanies] = useState([]);",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "  const searchInputRef = useRef(null);",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "    name: '',",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "    referenceNo: '',",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "    code: '',",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "    parentId: '',",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "    email: '',",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "    phone: '',",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "    address: {",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "      line1: '',",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "      line2: '',",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "      city: '',",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "      state: '',",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "      zip: '',",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "      country: ''",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "    },",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "    taxId: '',",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "    vatNumber: '',",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "    website: '',",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "    isActive: true",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "  });",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "  useEffect(() =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "    fetchBranches();",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "    fetchParentCompanies();",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "  }, []);",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "  // Ctrl+K keyboard shortcut to focus search",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "  useEffect(() =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "    const handleKeyDown = (e) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "      if ((e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey) &amp;&amp; e.key === 'k') {",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "        e.preventDefault();",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "        if (searchInputRef.current) {",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "          searchInputRef.current.focus();",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "      }",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "    };",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "    window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "    return () =&gt; window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);",
            "lineNumber": 52

        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "async function processRowForPreview(row, rowNum, existingCompaniesMap, csvCompaniesMap = null, existingUsersMap = null, existingCompaniesByIdMap = null, existingContactEmailsMap = null) {",

          },

          },

          },
          {
            "text": "          } else {",

          },
          {
            "text": "            result.warnings.push(&#96;Parent company with account number ${parentRefNum} not found in existing companies or import file. Will need to be created first or import may fail.&#96;);",

          },

          },

          },
          {

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\backend\\routes\\companies.js",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1179,

          },
          "endPosition": {

          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "// Get all related companies (ancestors and descendants) in nested structure",
            "lineNumber": 1182
          },
          {
            "text": "router.get('/:id/relationships', auth, async (req, res) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 1183
          },
          {
            "text": "  try {",
            "lineNumber": 1184
          },
          {
            "text": "    const company = await Company.findByPk(req.params.id, {",
            "lineNumber": 1185
          },
          {
            "text": "      include: [{",
            "lineNumber": 1186
          },
          {
            "text": "        model: Company,",
            "lineNumber": 1187
          },
          {
            "text": "        as: 'parent',",
            "lineNumber": 1188
          },
          {
            "text": "        required: false",
            "lineNumber": 1189
          },
          {
            "text": "      }]",
            "lineNumber": 1190
          },
          {
            "text": "    });",
            "lineNumber": 1191
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 1192
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (!company) {",
            "lineNumber": 1193
          },
          {
            "text": "      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Company not found' });",
            "lineNumber": 1194
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 1195
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 1196
          },
          {
            "text": "    // Get all ancestors (parent chain)",
            "lineNumber": 1197
          },
          {
            "text": "    const ancestors = [];",
            "lineNumber": 1198
          },
          {
            "text": "    let currentParent = company.parent;",
            "lineNumber": 1199
          },
          {
            "text": "    while (currentParent) {",
            "lineNumber": 1200
          },
          {
            "text": "      const parentWithParent = await Company.findByPk(currentParent.id, {",
            "lineNumber": 1201
          },
          {
            "text": "        include: [{",
            "lineNumber": 1202
          },
          {
            "text": "          model: Company,",
            "lineNumber": 1203
          },
          {
            "text": "          as: 'parent',",
            "lineNumber": 1204
          },
          {
            "text": "          required: false",
            "lineNumber": 1205
          },
          {
            "text": "        }]",
            "lineNumber": 1206
          },
          {
            "text": "      });",
            "lineNumber": 1207
          },
          {
            "text": "      ancestors.unshift(parentWithParent); // Add to beginning to maintain order",
            "lineNumber": 1208
          },
          {
            "text": "      currentParent = parentWithParent.parent;",
            "lineNumber": 1209
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 1210
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 1211
          },
          {
            "text": "    // Get all descendants (children recursively)",
            "lineNumber": 1212
          },
          {
            "text": "    const getChildrenRecursive = async (parentId, level = 0) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 1213
          },
          {
            "text": "      const children = await Company.findAll({",
            "lineNumber": 1214
          },
          {
            "text": "        where: { parentId },",
            "lineNumber": 1215
          },
          {
            "text": "        order: [['name', 'ASC']]",
            "lineNumber": 1216
          },
          {
            "text": "      });",
            "lineNumber": 1217
          },
          {
            "text": "      ",
            "lineNumber": 1218
          },
          {
            "text": "      const result = [];",
            "lineNumber": 1219
          },
          {
            "text": "      for (const child of children) {",
            "lineNumber": 1220
          },
          {
            "text": "        const childWithChildren = {",
            "lineNumber": 1221
          },
          {
            "text": "          id: child.id,",
            "lineNumber": 1222
          },
          {
            "text": "          name: child.name,",
            "lineNumber": 1223
          },
          {
            "text": "          type: child.type,",
            "lineNumber": 1224
          },
          {
            "text": "          referenceNo: child.referenceNo,",
            "lineNumber": 1225
          },
          {
            "text": "          code: child.code,",
            "lineNumber": 1226
          },
          {
            "text": "          isActive: child.isActive,",
            "lineNumber": 1227
          },
          {
            "text": "          level: level,",
            "lineNumber": 1228
          },
          {
            "text": "          children: await getChildrenRecursive(child.id, level + 1)",
            "lineNumber": 1229
          },
          {
            "text": "        };",
            "lineNumber": 1230
          },
          {
            "text": "        result.push(childWithChildren);",
            "lineNumber": 1231
          },
          {
            "text": "      }",
            "lineNumber": 1232
          },
          {
            "text": "      return result;",
            "lineNumber": 1233
          },
          {
            "text": "    };",
            "lineNumber": 1234
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 1235
          },
          {
            "text": "    const descendants = await getChildrenRecursive(company.id);",
            "lineNumber": 1236
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 1237
          },
          {
            "text": "    res.json({",
            "lineNumber": 1238
          },
          {
            "text": "      company: {",
            "lineNumber": 1239
          },
          {
            "text": "        id: company.id,",
            "lineNumber": 1240
          },
          {
            "text": "        name: company.name",
            "lineNumber": 1241
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1273
          },
          {
            "text": "}",

          }
        ]

    {

        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "// Get companies in hierarchical structure for tree view filters",

          },
          {
            "text": "// Only returns companies the user has access to, structured as a tree",
            "lineNumber": 197
          },
          {
            "text": "router.get('/hierarchy', auth, checkDocumentAccess, async (req, res) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 198
          },
          {
            "text": "  try {",
            "lineNumber": 199

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

There are no migrations in the repo, so the actual DB constraint (RESTRICT vs CASCADE) isn't defined in code I can see. In Sequelize, when you don't specify `onDelete` on a belongsTo/hasMany, the database may still have a foreign key. Typically PostgreSQL defaults to NO ACTION (prevent delete if referenced). So when you call `company.destroy()`, the database would likely throw a foreign key violation if any rows in invoices, credit_notes, statements, document_queries, user_companies, email_logs reference that company.

So the practical answer is:
1. The **delete route** only checks for **child companies**. It does **not** check for invoices, credit notes, statements, document queries, user-company assignments, or email logs.
2. When `company.destroy()` runs, if the database has FK constraints with RESTRICT (or NO ACTION), the delete will **fail** with a foreign key error when any of those related rows exist.
3. If the DB had CASCADE, documents would be deleted (or SET NULL if the column allows null).
4. The Sequelize associations don't set onDelete, so behavior is whatever the DB schema has.

I should also check if there's a File model that has companyId, since unallocated/files were mentioned.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

Invoice.companyId is allowNull: false, so if the DB has a FK and it's RESTRICT/NO ACTION, deleting a company that has invoices would fail. The application code does not explicitly prevent deletion when documents exist—it only checks for child companies. So the actual behavior depends on the database constraints. I'll summarize what the code does and what typically happens.
</details></think>

---


Here’s what the codebase does and what that implies for documents when a company is deleted.

---

## What the delete route does

In **`backend/routes/companies.js`** (around 1090–1138), company delete:

1. Loads the company.
2. Checks only for **child companies** (`parentId = company.id`). If any exist, it returns **400** with:  
   *"Cannot delete company with children. Please delete or reassign children first."*
3. Calls **`company.destroy()`**.
4. Queues a nested set update and logs the deletion.

There is **no check** for:

- Invoices  
- Credit notes  
- Statements  
- Document queries  
- User–company assignments (`user_companies`)  
- Email logs  

So at the application level, the only guard is “no child companies.”

---

## What happens to documents when delete runs

Documents are linked to the company by **`companyId`** (and similar FKs in other tables). The associations in **`backend/models/index.js`** (e.g. 99–106, 123–124) do **not** set `onDelete`. So behavior is determined by the **database** foreign key constraints, not by Sequelize in this codebase.

- **If the DB uses RESTRICT / NO ACTION (typical):**  
  As soon as **any** row references the company (e.g. an invoice with that `companyId`), **`company.destroy()` will throw a foreign key error** and the delete will fail. The API would return a 500 with that error. **No documents are deleted or updated** in that case.

- **If the DB used CASCADE:**  
  Deleting the company would also delete all dependent rows (invoices, credit notes, etc.). The app code does not implement or encourage this.

- **If the DB used SET NULL:**  
  Only possible where the column is nullable. In the models, **`Invoice.companyId`** (and similar) are **not** nullable, so at least those tables cannot use SET NULL for `companyId` without a schema change.

So in the current design (and with normal FK constraints), **documents are not explicitly handled on company delete**; the delete either **fails** when any document (or other related row) exists, or the DB would have to be explicitly set up to CASCADE/SET NULL.

---

## Related data that references the company

From the models and routes, these reference the company and can block or be affected by delete, depending on DB constraints:

| Relation | Model / table | Typical effect if FK is RESTRICT |
|----------|----------------|-----------------------------------|
| Invoices | `Invoice.companyId` | Delete blocked |
| Credit notes | `CreditNote.companyId` | Delete blocked |
| Statements | `Statement.companyId` | Delete blocked |
| Document queries | `DocumentQuery.companyId` | Delete blocked |
| User assignments | `UserCompany.companyId` | Delete blocked |
| Email logs | `EmailLog.companyId` | Delete blocked |

Files are linked via **`File.customerId`** → `Customer`, not directly to `Company` in the model, though in the app “customer” is often used in a company context for unallocated flows.

---

## Summary

Summary of what was done:

**Backend (`backend/utils/permissions.js`)**
- **INVOICES_EDIT** is now allowed only for: `global_admin`, `administrator`, `manager`.
- **CREDIT_NOTES_EDIT** is now allowed only for: `global_admin`, `administrator`, `manager`.

**Frontend**
- No code changes. The Edit button is already shown only when the user has the right permission:
  - **Invoices** (`Invoices.js`): `{hasPermission('INVOICES_EDIT') && ( ... Edit button ... )}`
  - **Credit notes** (`CreditNotes.js`): `{hasPermission('CREDIT_NOTES_EDIT') && ( ... Edit button ... )}`
- The edit routes are already protected with `PermissionRoute` and `INVOICES_EDIT` / `CREDIT_NOTES_EDIT`, so users without the permission are blocked even if they open the edit URL directly.

**Result**
- **Global Admin, Administrator, Manager**: Can edit invoices and credit notes; they see the Edit button and can use the edit pages/APIs.
- **All other roles** (e.g. credit_senior, credit_controller, external_user, notification_contact): Do not have `INVOICES_EDIT` or `CREDIT_NOTES_EDIT`, so the Edit button is hidden and the backend returns 403 if they call the edit endpoints.

_**User**_

Can we ensure that if a Company is deleted NO DOCUMENTS are deleted. They just remained assigned to the company. That way if the company is ever re-added the documents are still there?

_**Agent (model claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user wants to ensure that when a company is deleted:
1. NO documents (invoices, credit notes, statements, etc.) are deleted
2. Documents remain assigned to the company (i.e. keep their companyId pointing to the deleted company)
3. So if the company is ever re-added (presumably they mean re-created or restored), the documents would still be there

There are a few ways to interpret "re-added":
- Soft delete: Don't actually delete the company row; just mark it as deleted (e.g. deletedAt, isActive = false). Then "re-adding" could mean reactivating. Documents would still have companyId pointing to that row.
- Hard delete but keep documents: Actually delete the company row, but set documents' companyId to NULL or leave them as orphaned (companyId still points to non-existent ID). If company is "re-added" as a new company with same details, the documents would need to be reassigned - they wouldn't automatically link.

The user said "documents remain assigned to the company" and "if the company is ever re-added the documents are still there". That strongly suggests they want the company to NOT be actually removed from the database - i.e. soft delete. So when we "delete" a company we:
1. Don't actually destroy the row
2. Mark it as deleted/inactive so it doesn't show in normal lists
3. Documents keep their companyId → the company row still exists, so they're still "assigned"
4. If we "re-add" (restore) the company, we just unmark it as deleted - documents are already there

Alternatively they might mean: when we hard-delete the company, the DB should NOT cascade delete documents. So documents would have companyId pointing to a deleted company ID (orphaned FK). But then "re-adding" the company would create a NEW id, so documents wouldn't be linked unless we had some other key (e.g. referenceNo) to match. So the clean approach is soft delete.

Let me check the current behavior:
1. Company delete route - we found it only checks for child companies, then calls company.destroy(). So it's a hard delete.
2. If the DB has FK with RESTRICT, the delete would fail when documents exist (we established that earlier).
3. So currently: either delete fails (if FKs are RESTRICT), or if CASCADE then documents get deleted.

User wants: when company is "deleted", no documents are deleted and they stay assigned. So we need to:
1. Implement soft delete for companies: e.g. set isActive = false and maybe a deletedAt timestamp. Don't call destroy().
2. Filter out soft-deleted companies from normal listing/selection (parents, for-assignment, hierarchy, etc.) - but they might already filter by isActive in many places.
3. When "deleting", just deactivate (and set deletedAt if we add it). Optionally prevent any new documents being assigned to the company.
4. If they want "re-add", we could have a "restore" that sets isActive = true and clears deletedAt.

Let me check if Company model has isActive, deletedAt, or similar, and how the delete route and lists work.
</details></think>

---


Summary of what was implemented:

### 1. **Soft delete (no documents removed)**  
`DELETE /api/companies/:id` now **deactivates** the company instead of removing it:

- Sets `company.isActive = false` and saves (no `destroy()`).
- **No documents are deleted.** Invoices, credit notes, statements, etc. keep their `companyId` and stay linked to the company row.
- The “no children” rule is unchanged: you still can’t delete a company that has child companies (you must delete/deactivate children first).
- If the company is already inactive, the API returns 400 with a clear message.
- The activity log records that the company was deactivated and that documents are retained.

### 2. **Default list behaviour**  
`GET /api/companies` (list) now **defaults to active companies only** when `isActive` is not in the query:

- So “deleted” (inactive) companies are hidden from the normal list.
- Callers can still get inactive companies with `GET /api/companies?isActive=false` (e.g. for an admin “deleted companies” view or restore flow).

### 3. **Restore (re-add) endpoint**  
`PUT /api/companies/:id/restore`:

- Requires `COMPANIES_EDIT` (same as edit).
- Sets `isActive = true` for that company.
- All documents that were left assigned to the company are again associated with an active company, so “re-adding” is just reactivating the same record.

**Flow:**

- **Delete company** → Company is marked inactive; documents stay assigned; company disappears from default lists.
- **Re-add company** → Call `PUT /api/companies/:id/restore` for that company (or add a “Restore” action in the UI that calls it). The company becomes active again and all existing documents are still linked.

No database migrations were added; the existing `isActive` flag is used. If you want, we can add a “Show deleted companies” filter and a “Restore” button on the Companies page that use `?isActive=false` and the restore endpoint.

---

o	Delete Users
o	Import Companies, by Add Company button + Import Data
o	Import Users, by Add User button + Import Data
o	Upload Invoices
o	Upload Credit Notes

Can we scan the code and plan for this carefully please?

Here is the full analysis. I've cross-referenced every requested action against both the **backend** permission/role checks and the **frontend** button visibility logic.

---

## Current State vs. What You Want

| # | Action | Backend permission | Manager has it? | Frontend gate | Manager sees it? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Delete Companies** | `COMPANIES_DELETE` | **Yes** already | `isAdministrator()` = GA or Admin only | **No** - button hidden |
| 2 | **Delete Users** | `USERS_DELETE` | **No** - GA, Admin only | No `hasPermission` check; delete button always shows (line ~1373) unless self or admin-on-admin | **Yes** (button shows but API returns 403) |
| 3 | **Import Companies** (via Import Data page) | Inline check: `global_admin` or `administrator` only (lines 1689, 1938) | **No** | `canAccessImportData()` includes manager (can see page); but company import API will 403 | **Partial** - can see page but import fails |
| 4 | **Import Users** (via Import Data page) | `canManageUsers` middleware (manager already passes); no extra `USERS_IMPORT` check on the route | **Yes** - `canManageUsers` passes for manager | Same as #3 — page is visible | **Yes** |
| 5 | **Upload Invoices** | `INVOICES_IMPORT` | **No** - GA, Admin only | Inline role check: GA, Admin, Manager, Staff (line ~936) | **Yes** (button shows but API returns 403) |
| 6 | **Upload Credit Notes** | `CREDIT_NOTES_IMPORT` | **No** - GA, Admin only | `hasPermission('CREDIT_NOTES_IMPORT')` | **No** - button hidden |

---

## Detailed Change Plan

### File 1: `backend/utils/permissions.js` (4 changes)

| Permission | Current | Change to |
|---|---|---|
| `INVOICES_IMPORT` (line 46) | `['global_admin', 'administrator']` | `['global_admin', 'administrator', 'manager']` |
| `CREDIT_NOTES_IMPORT` (line 55) | `['global_admin', 'administrator']` | `['global_admin', 'administrator', 'manager']` |
| `USERS_DELETE` (line 102) | `['global_admin', 'administrator']` | `['global_admin', 'administrator', 'manager']` |
| `IMPORT_DATA_MANAGE` (line 29) | `['global_admin']` | `['global_admin', 'administrator', 'manager']` |

(Note: `COMPANIES_DELETE` is **already** `['global_admin', 'administrator', 'manager']` — no change needed there.)

### File 2: `frontend/src/context/PermissionContext.js` (4 matching changes)

Same four permissions updated to match the backend (lines 28, 41, 48, 85).

### File 3: `backend/routes/companies.js` (2 inline role checks)

These endpoints use **inline** role checks, not the permission system:

- **Line ~1689** (`POST /import/preview`): `if (req.user.role !== 'global_admin' && req.user.role !== 'administrator')` — add `&& req.user.role !== 'manager'`
- **Line ~1938** (`POST /import`): same inline check — add `&& req.user.role !== 'manager'`
- **Line ~477** (company export): same pattern — add `&& req.user.role !== 'manager'`

### File 4: `frontend/src/pages/Companies.js` (3 hardcoded checks)

The `isAdministrator()` function (line 605) returns `true` only for GA and Admin. It gates:
- **Delete button** per-row (line ~1561): `{isAdministrator() && ( ... Delete ... )}`
- **Deactivate/Activate button** per-row (line ~1561): same block
- **Bulk actions dropdown** (line ~1312): `{isAdministrator() && selectedCompanyIds.length > 0 && ...}`
- **Assigned Users column** (lines ~1448, ~1521): `{(currentUser?.role === 'global_admin' || currentUser?.role === 'administrator') && ...}`

**Fix**: Update `isAdministrator()` to also include `manager`, or replace these checks with `hasPermission('COMPANIES_DELETE')` where appropriate (the delete/deactivate buttons) and add manager to the assigned-users visibility.

### File 5: `frontend/src/pages/UserManagement.js` (1 hardcoded check)

- **Pending Accounts button** (line ~1177): `{(currentUser?.role === 'global_admin' || currentUser?.role === 'administrator') && ...}` — add `|| currentUser?.role === 'manager'` (we already allowed managers in the backend for pending accounts earlier).

### File 6: `backend/routes/users.js` (1 potential safeguard)

- The delete route (line ~1403) uses `requirePermission('USERS_DELETE')` which will work once the permission is updated.
- There's also a check: "Only Global Admins can delete Administrators" (line ~1453). This stays as-is — Managers could delete users **below** their level (credit_senior, credit_controller, external_user, etc.) but not Administrators. The `canManageRole()` check (line ~1440) already enforces this hierarchy.

### File 7: `frontend/src/pages/Invoices.js` (already fine)

- Upload button (line ~936) already includes `manager` in the inline role check. No change needed.

### File 8: `frontend/src/pages/ImportData.js` (no code change)

- `canAccessImportData()` (line ~23) already includes `manager`. The page is already visible.
- Company/user import type selector is shown for anyone who can access the page. The actual import API will now allow manager once the backend is updated.

---

## Summary

**Backend changes (2 files):**
- `backend/utils/permissions.js` — add `'manager'` to 4 permissions
- `backend/routes/companies.js` — add `'manager'` to 3 inline role checks (import preview, import, export)

**Frontend changes (3 files):**
- `frontend/src/context/PermissionContext.js` — add `'manager'` to same 4 permissions
- `frontend/src/pages/Companies.js` — update `isAdministrator()` or individual checks to include manager for Delete, Deactivate, Bulk Actions, and Assigned Users column
- `frontend/src/pages/UserManagement.js` — add manager to the Pending Accounts button visibility

**No changes needed:**
- `COMPANIES_DELETE` permission — already includes manager
- `Invoices.js` upload button — already includes manager
- `ImportData.js` page access — already includes manager
- `roleHierarchy.js` / `canManageUsers` — already works for manager
- User delete backend route — permission update is sufficient; role hierarchy prevents managers from deleting admins

Now that we've removed credit_senior from UNALLOCATED_REALLOCATE, this route would no longer allow credit_senior to save changes. But the user said credit_senior and credit_controller should not be able to edit - they should be view-only. So this is correct behavior.

However, the route comment says "GA + Admin + Manager + Credit Senior" - I should update the comment too. And the permission used is UNALLOCATED_REALLOCATE which means "can reallocate" - but this is a save/edit operation. It should probably use UNALLOCATED_EDIT. 

I'm realizing the PUT endpoint is specifically for saving edits to the parsed data, while the POST endpoint handles the allocation attempt. Since we want credit_senior and credit_controller to have view-only access with no edit or allocation capabilities, switching to UNALLOCATED_EDIT makes sense and aligns with the actual operation being performed.</details></think>

All done and pushed. Here's a summary of what was changed:

---

**Permissions updated** (both backend `permissions.js` and frontend `PermissionContext.js`):

| Permission | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `UNALLOCATED_EDIT` | GA, Admin | GA, Admin, **Manager** |
| `UNALLOCATED_REALLOCATE` | GA, Admin, Manager, **Credit Senior** | GA, Admin, Manager |

`credit_senior` was **removed** from `UNALLOCATED_REALLOCATE`. `UNALLOCATED_VIEW` and `UNALLOCATED_DOWNLOAD` remain unchanged — Credit Senior and Credit Controller can still see and download documents.

---

**Backend route guards** (`backend/routes/unallocated.js`):
- `PUT /:id` (save/edit parsed data) — changed from `UNALLOCATED_REALLOCATE` to `UNALLOCATED_EDIT` (was allowing Credit Senior to edit)
- `POST /:id/attempt-allocation` — **added** `requirePermission('UNALLOCATED_REALLOCATE')` (previously had **no** permission check beyond view access)
- `POST /bulk-allocate` — already had `UNALLOCATED_REALLOCATE` (now correct since credit_senior removed)

---

**Frontend — Unallocated list page** (`Unallocated.js`):
- **"Allocate Selected"** button — now only shown if user has `UNALLOCATED_REALLOCATE` permission
- **"Allocate All"** button — same, switched from hardcoded role check to `hasPermission('UNALLOCATED_REALLOCATE')`
- **"Delete Selected"** and per-row **"Delete"** buttons — now gated by `hasPermission('UNALLOCATED_DELETE')`
- **"Clear All"** button — same, now uses `hasPermission('UNALLOCATED_DELETE')`

---

**Frontend — UnallocatedView page** (`UnallocatedView.js`):
- All form fields (Document Type, Account Number, Date, Invoice Number, PO Number, Total, VAT, Delivery Address, Goods Amount, Invoice To) are **disabled and read-only** for Credit Senior and Credit Controller
- **"Save Changes"** button — hidden unless user has `UNALLOCATED_EDIT` permission
- **"Attempt Allocation"** button — hidden unless user has `UNALLOCATED_REALLOCATE` permission
- The "Cancel" button text changes to "Back" for read-only users

I can't find a definitive code bug that would cause the first email to succeed but subsequent ones to fail, but I found an important issue: **errors during the auto-send in `auth.js` are silently swallowed** — the user sees "Verification code sent to your email" even when the email send actually fails (line 315-317).

This is most likely what's happening: the email send is failing on subsequent attempts (possibly due to an Office 365 issue, rate limiting, or transient error), but the error is hidden from the user.

Let me fix this by surfacing the failure and making the resend more robust.

Done. Here's what I found and fixed:

---

**Root cause**: The email send in `auth.js` was wrapped in a silent `try/catch` — if the email failed to send (Office 365 error, transient network issue, etc.), the error was caught and logged to the console but the user was **still told** "Verification code sent to your email." They'd sit there waiting for an email that was never sent.

**Changes made:**

1. **`backend/routes/auth.js`** — Added **retry logic** (2 attempts with 1s delay) for the 2FA email auto-send during login. If both attempts fail, the response now includes `emailSendFailed: true` and the message changes to "Failed to send verification code. Please use the resend button."

2. **`backend/routes/twoFactor.js`** — Added the same retry logic (2 attempts) to the `/send-email-code` resend endpoint, and improved the error message returned on failure.

3. **`frontend/src/context/AuthContext.js`** — Passes the `emailSendFailed` flag through from the login response.

4. **`frontend/src/pages/Login.js`** — Passes `emailSendFailed` in the navigation state to the TwoFactorVerify page.

5. **`frontend/src/pages/TwoFactorVerify.js`** — Shows a **yellow warning banner** ("Email could not be sent. Please click 'Resend' below to try again.") when `emailSendFailed` is true, plus a toast notification.

---

**Next step to diagnose further**: After deploying, if the issue happens again, check the server console logs for the actual error message — it will now log `Failed to send 2FA email (attempt 1/2): <actual error>`. This will tell you exactly what Office 365 is returning (e.g., throttling, authentication issue, etc.).

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is showing me the PM2 logs from their production server. Let me analyze what's happening:

1. The backend just restarted (PM2 had to SIGKILL it and restart)
2. There are Redis connection errors from Feb 28 (ECONNREFUSED on port 6379) - but those seem to be old errors
3. The queue worker shows Redis is now connected successfully
4. The backend logs show a login attempt for `robeccles@fastmail.com` that succeeded (password match: true)
5. The last line shows `Settings from cache: onlyExternalUsersChangeDocumentStatus = true`

But crucially, the logs cut off RIGHT after the password check. 

The login succeeded with the password match, but I can't see what happened next with the 2FA email send since the logs only show the last 15 lines. Looking at the timestamps, the backend restarted at 17:11:10 and the login attempt happened a few minutes later at 17:14:21-22. The user likely just deployed the latest code and is testing the login flow.

I'm also noticing some database schema issues in the queue worker logs—missing columns like `File.invoiceId` and `User.deletedAt`—but the main concern is that I need to see more of the backend logs to determine whether the 2FA email actually sent. Plus, those debug logs are exposing sensitive password information that shouldn't be in production.

Looking at the auth flow and the settings cache log appearing at 17:14:22, that's coming from the 2FA requirements check, which happens right after password verification and before the email would be sent. The output got cut off before showing whether the email send succeeded.</details></think>

---

I can see the logs from your production server. A few observations:

**The login is working** — the password check for `robeccles@fastmail.com` succeeded (`bcrypt.compare result: true`). The settings are being loaded from cache. But the PM2 log is only showing the **last 15 lines**, so the 2FA email send result is cut off.

To see what actually happened with the 2FA email, you'll need more lines. Try:

```bash
pm2 logs invoice-portal-backend --lines 100
```

Or to see it live as you attempt a login:

```bash
pm2 logs invoice-portal-backend --raw
```

Then try logging in with the email 2FA in another browser tab. You should now see one of:
- A success message from the email send
- `Failed to send 2FA email (attempt 1/2): <actual error>` — this is the new logging we just added

**Other issues I spotted in the logs:**

1. **`column File.invoiceId does not exist`** — the queue worker is referencing a column that doesn't exist in the database. This is causing orphaned file cleanup to fail nightly.

2. **`column User.deletedAt does not exist`** — the admin notification sender is failing because it expects a `deletedAt` column (paranoid/soft-delete) that isn't on the User model.

3. **Redis was down on Feb 28** (`ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:6379`) — but it looks like it's connected now as of the latest restart.

Can you run the login test with `--lines 100` and share what the logs say about the 2FA email send?

---

This is very helpful. I can see the key information now:

**Important finding**: At 17:02, the email transporter log shows:
```
[EmailService] Cleaned up idle transporter: smtp:smtp.vsl-net.com:25: (2 messages sent)
```

Your email is going through **SMTP on port 25** to `smtp.vsl-net.com`, not Office 365. And the login at 17:14 happened AFTER the server restarted at 17:11, which means the old SMTP connection pool was destroyed — a fresh transporter would need to be created.

But the critical observation is: **after the `Settings from cache` log at 17:14:22, there's absolutely no further output** — no email send attempt, no error, nothing. This means one of two things:

1. The new code (with retry + error logging) **hasn't been deployed yet** — the server restarted at 17:11 but was likely running the old code where errors are silently swallowed
2. OR the `requires2FA` check evaluated to `false` for this login attempt and 2FA was skipped entirely

Have you pulled and rebuilt with the latest commit? If not, on the server run:

```bash
cd /var/www/makita-invportal
git pull
cd frontend && npm run build
cd ../backend && pm2 restart all
```

Then try logging in again and check:

```bash
pm2 logs invoice-portal-backend --lines 50
```

The new code will log `Failed to send 2FA email (attempt 1/2): <actual error>` if the email fails, so we'll see exactly what's going wrong. If the email succeeds silently, it points to a mail delivery issue with `smtp.vsl-net.com` rather than a code bug.

---

The logs confirm PM2 is still running the old code — there's zero output from the new retry/logging code after the settings check. Even though `git pull` says "up to date" (the files are on disk), PM2 needs to be restarted to load the new code into memory:

```bash
pm2 restart invoice-portal-backend
```

Then try logging in again and immediately check:

```bash
pm2 logs invoice-portal-backend --lines 30
```

But I also notice something important from the logs: after both login attempts (17:14 and 17:18), there's **only one** `Settings from cache` log each time, then nothing. If the code were entering the 2FA email branch, there'd be a **second** `Settings from cache` log (from the `Settings.getSettings()` call inside the email send block). This means either:

1. 2FA is **not required** in your settings (`settings.twoFactorAuth.required` is `false`), so the login completes without 2FA
2. OR the user `robeccles@fastmail.com` already has 2FA set up with the **authenticator** method, not email

Can you clarify: when you say "the emails get sent once but not after that" — is this happening during:
- **(A)** The initial 2FA setup (choosing "Email" method for the first time)?
- **(B)** Subsequent logins after email 2FA is already configured?
- **(C)** Clicking "Resend" on the verification code screen?

And for testing, make sure you restart PM2 first so we get the new diagnostic logging.

Fixed and pushed. The error was:

```
ReferenceError: Cannot access 'T' before initialization at TwoFactorVerify.js:44
```

My `useEffect` at line 40 referenced `isEmailMethod`, but that `const` isn't declared until line 186. JavaScript `const` declarations are hoisted but not initialized, so accessing them before their declaration throws a `ReferenceError`. The fix uses `twoFactorMethod === 'email'` directly instead.

(All the `runtime.lastError` / `BG.js` errors are from a browser extension — not related to the app.)

On the server, pull and rebuild the frontend:

```bash
cd /var/www/makita-invportal
git pull
cd frontend && npm run build
pm2 restart invoice-portal-backend
```

Then try the 2FA email login again.

---

I can now see what's happening. Your login at 17:23:31 shows exactly one `Settings from cache` log, then nothing else — **no email send attempt at all**. This means the 2FA code path IS being entered (since the frontend shows "Redirecting to 2FA verify"), but `twoFactorMethod` for your `robeccles@fastmail.com` account is **`authenticator`**, not **`email`**.

The code at line 285 in auth.js:
```javascript
const twoFactorMethod = user.twoFactorMethod || 'authenticator';
```

If `user.twoFactorMethod` is `null` or `'authenticator'`, the entire email-sending block is skipped. No email is attempted because the backend thinks this account uses an authenticator app, not email codes.

You can verify this by checking the database:

```bash
cd /var/www/makita-invportal/backend
node -e "const {User} = require('./models'); User.findOne({where:{email:'robeccles@fastmail.com'}}).then(u => { console.log('twoFactorMethod:', u.twoFactorMethod); console.log('twoFactorEnabled:', u.twoFactorEnabled); console.log('twoFactorVerified:', u.twoFactorVerified); process.exit(); })"
```

If it shows `twoFactorMethod: null` or `twoFactorMethod: 'authenticator'`, that's why no email is being sent. To test email 2FA, you'd need to either:
1. Reset 2FA for this account (set `twoFactorEnabled`, `twoFactorVerified` to `false`, `twoFactorMethod` to `null`) and then choose "Email" during setup
2. Or test with a different account that already has `twoFactorMethod: 'email'`

```javascript

```

So the backend is treating this as an authenticator-based 2FA account, and never sending any email. The user's account was likely set up with the authenticator method originally (or the method was never properly saved).

The user wants to use email 2FA. They need to either:

2. Or update the database directly

I should recommend resetting 2FA and going through setup again to select "Email" this time, or they could update the method directly in the database if they want a quicker fix. ---

There it is. `twoFactorMethod: null` — the code defaults to `'authenticator'` when the method is null:

```javascript
const twoFactorMethod = user.twoFactorMethod || 'authenticator';
```

So the backend has been treating your account as an authenticator-based 2FA account this whole time. No email was ever attempted on login. The 2FA was likely set up before the email method was added, so the `twoFactorMethod` column was never populated.

To test email 2FA, reset your account's 2FA and go through setup again, choosing "Email":

```bash
node -e "const {User} = require('./models'); User.findOne({where:{email:'robeccles@fastmail.com'}}).then(u => { u.twoFactorEnabled = false; u.twoFactorVerified = false; u.twoFactorMethod = null; u.twoFactorSecret = null; u.emailTwoFactorCode = null; u.emailTwoFactorExpires = null; return u.save(); }).then(() => { console.log('2FA reset successfully'); process.exit(); })"
```

Then log in again — you should be prompted to choose your 2FA method (Authenticator App or Email). Choose **Email**, and you should receive the verification code. Once you complete setup, `twoFactorMethod` will be saved as `'email'`, and subsequent logins will auto-send the email code.

This also explains "emails get sent once but not after that" — the first email came from the `setup-email` endpoint (which worked), but the setup might not have been completed successfully, leaving `twoFactorMethod` as null. On the next login, the code defaulted to authenticator and never sent any email.

1. Replace `window.confirm()` dialogs for user deletion (both single and bulk) with proper modals similar to the Invoice delete modals

Let me also check if the backend user delete route needs a bulk delete endpoint or if it handles reason:

Currently:
- Single user delete: `handleDeleteUser(userId)` uses `window.confirm()` then `api.delete(`/api/users/${userId}`)`


- Bulk user delete: `handleBulkDelete()` uses `window.confirm()` then loops through individual delete calls

I need to add modal state management and UI components for both single and bulk deletion, update the handlers to capture the deletion reason, and check whether the backend has a dedicated bulk delete endpoint or if I should create one instead of looping through individual calls.</details></think>
